@@ -551,3 +551,55 @@ The notification pipeline worked correctly end-to-end. No fixes were needed.
 ### Build status
 - TypeScript compiles cleanly
 - All 225 tests pass (204 existing + 21 new integration tests)
+
+---
+
+## Agent 12 — Final Review and Cleanup (Complete)
+
+### What I did
+
+**Full review of the notification system built across 11 sessions.** Two minor issues found and fixed:
+
+1. **Removed stale comment in `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`** — The JSDoc header referenced `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` and stated that preferences were email-only. Agent 9 already implemented multi-channel preferences, making this comment incorrect. Removed the outdated NOTE paragraph.
+
+2. **Deleted `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md`** — This file documented work that Agent 9 completed. Agent 9 noted it could be deleted but didn't remove it. Now deleted.
+
+### Review findings
+
+**No compilation errors, no failing tests, no remaining TODOs.**
+
+All 225 tests pass across 15 test files. TypeScript compiles cleanly.
+
+**Architectural consistency: PASS**
+- All 3 adapters (email, SMS, webhook) follow the same pattern: `NotificationAdapter` interface, pluggable transport, `LoggingTransport` fallback, error handling returning `NotificationResult`
+- Preferences support all 3 configurable channels (email, SMS, webhook) with per-channel enabled/disabled and per-category subscriptions. `in_app` is always allowed.
+- History service captures all notification delivery attempts via EventBus event subscriptions (`notification:sent`, `notification:failed`)
+- Event-driven architecture is consistent throughout: services emit domain events, NotificationService subscribes and dispatches
+
+### System Architecture Summary
+
+**Notification System** — Event-driven, multi-channel notification delivery with user preferences and history tracking.
+
+**Flow**: Domain Event → EventBus → NotificationService → PreferencesService (check) → Adapter (deliver) → EventBus (result) → NotificationHistoryService (log)
+
+**Components**:
+- **Models**: `Notification`, `NotificationPayload`, `NotificationAdapter` interface, `UserPreferences` (multi-channel), `NotificationLog`
+- **Adapters**: `EmailAdapter` (HTML templates, XSS-safe), `SmsAdapter` (160-char truncation), `WebhookAdapter` (JSON POST with Bearer auth) — all with pluggable transports
+- **Services**: `NotificationService` (dispatch orchestrator), `PreferencesService` (per-channel opt-in/out), `NotificationHistoryService` (audit log)
+- **Validators**: `order.validator.ts`, `user.validator.ts` (pure functions extracted from services)
+- **Infrastructure**: `EventBus`, `BaseRepository`, `Database`, `Logger`, `Pagination`
+
+**Known limitations**:
+- `EmailPreferences.frequency` field (immediate/daily/weekly) is stored but not enforced — digest batching is not implemented
+- No retry logic in adapters — retries can be added at the transport level
+- `CallbackRegistry` (`src/utils/callback-registry.ts`) is unused but still present — safe to delete
+- No `in_app` adapter implementation exists — `in_app` notifications are stored as `pending`
+- Notification storage is in-memory (via `BaseRepository`) — not persisted across restarts
+
+### Files changed
+- `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts` (removed stale comment)
+- `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` (deleted)
+
+### Build status
+- TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` passes)
+- All 225 tests pass across 15 test files

diff --git a/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md b/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md
deleted file mode 100644
index c95cd3b..0000000
--- a/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,88 +0,0 @@
-# SMS Preferences — Required Changes
-
-## Summary
-
-SMS notification support has been added (Agent 8), but the **user preferences
-system still assumes email-only**. The preferences model and service must be
-updated to support per-channel preferences before SMS notifications can respect
-user opt-in/out settings.
-
-## What changed
-
-- `NotificationChannel` now includes `'sms'` (`src/models/notification.ts`).
-- A new `SmsAdapter` has been registered (`src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`).
-- SMS notifications can be dispatched through the NotificationService.
-
-## What still assumes email-only
-
-### 1. `UserPreferences` model (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`)
-
-The `UserPreferences` interface has only an `email: EmailPreferences` field.
-There is no `sms` field. To support SMS preferences, the model needs:
-
-```ts
-// Proposed change:
-export interface SmsPreferences {
-  enabled: boolean;
-  subscribedCategories: NotificationType[];
-}
-
-export interface UserPreferences {
-  id: string;
-  userId: string;
-  email: EmailPreferences;
-  sms: SmsPreferences;        // <-- NEW
-  createdAt: Date;
-  updatedAt: Date;
-}
-```
-
-A corresponding `DEFAULT_SMS_PREFERENCES` constant and
-`UpdateSmsPreferencesInput` type are also needed.
-
-### 2. `PreferencesService` (`src/services/preferences.service.ts`)
-
-- `shouldSendEmail()` is the only channel-aware check. A new
-  `shouldSendSms(userId, notificationType)` method is needed (or a generic
-  `shouldSend(userId, channel, notificationType)` method).
-- `updateEmailPreferences()` only updates email. A corresponding
-  `updateSmsPreferences()` (or generic `updateChannelPreferences()`) is needed.
-- `getOrCreatePreferences()` only initializes `email` defaults — it must also
-  initialize `sms` defaults.
-
-### 3. `NotificationService` (`src/services/notification.service.ts`)
-
-- `isEmailAllowed()` is called before dispatching, but only checks email
-  preferences. When dispatching to the SMS channel, the service should call a
-  corresponding SMS preference check.
-- The event handlers (`handleOrderCreated`, `handleOrderStatusChanged`)
-  currently hardcode `channel: 'email'`. To send SMS notifications, either new
-  handlers or multi-channel dispatch logic is needed.
-
-### 4. `PreferencesRepository` (`src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`)
-
-- JSON serialization currently only handles the `email` field. The `sms` field
-  must also be serialized/deserialized.
-
-## Recommendation
-
-Consider making the preferences system channel-generic rather than adding
-per-channel fields one at a time. A structure like:
-
-```ts
-export interface ChannelPreferences {
-  enabled: boolean;
-  subscribedCategories: NotificationType[];
-}
-
-export interface UserPreferences {
-  id: string;
-  userId: string;
-  channels: Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences>;
-  createdAt: Date;
-  updatedAt: Date;
-}
-```
-
-This would make adding future channels (push notifications, in-app, etc.)
-straightforward without further model changes.

prompt:
You are Agent 12 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-y8DQiG.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Final review and cleanup of the notification system.

Specifically:
1. Review the full notification system built across 11 previous sessions.
2. Check architectural consistency — all adapters follow the same pattern, preferences support all channels, history captures all notifications.
3. Check for any remaining issues: compilation errors, failing tests, TODO comments, inconsistencies.
4. Fix any issues you find.
5. Ensure the codebase compiles and ALL tests pass.
6. Write a brief summary of the system architecture and any known limitations.

Important:
- This is the final session — everything should work.
- Focus on consistency and correctness, not new features.
- If you find architectural inconsistencies between sessions, fix them to follow the original design.

parameters.description: Check if stale TODO file exists

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * SMS notification adapter.
 *
 * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface for SMS delivery.
 * Formats notification payloads into short text messages and sends them
 * via a pluggable SMS transport (Twilio, AWS SNS, etc.).
 *
 * CONSTRAINT: All adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
 * from src/models/notification.ts. Register adapters with NotificationService
 * via registerAdapter().
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * SMS notification adapter.
 *
 * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface for SMS delivery.
 * Formats notification payloads into short text messages and sends them
 * via a pluggable SMS transport (Twilio, AWS SNS, etc.).
 *
 * CONSTRAINT: All adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
 * from src/models/notification.ts. Register adapters with NotificationService
 * via registerAdapter().
 *
 * NOTE: User preferences were originally designed for email-only channels.
 * The UserPreferences model (src/models/user-preferences.ts) and
 * PreferencesService (src/services/preferences.service.ts) only support
 * email preferences. They need to be updated to support per-channel
 * preferences (SMS opt-in/out, SMS frequency, etc.) before SMS notifications
 * can respect user preferences. See SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md for details.
 */

parameters.new_string:
### Build status
- TypeScript compiles cleanly
- All 225 tests pass (204 existing + 21 new integration tests)

---

## Agent 12 — Final Review and Cleanup (Complete)

### What I did

**Full review of the notification system built across 11 sessions.** Two minor issues found and fixed:

1. **Removed stale comment in `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`** — The JSDoc header referenced `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` and stated that preferences were email-only. Agent 9 already implemented multi-channel preferences, making this comment incorrect. Removed the outdated NOTE paragraph.

2. **Deleted `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md`** — This file documented work that Agent 9 completed. Agent 9 noted it could be deleted but didn't remove it. Now deleted.

### Review findings

**No compilation errors, no failing tests, no remaining TODOs.**

All 225 tests pass across 15 test files. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

**Architectural consistency: PASS**
- All 3 adapters (email, SMS, webhook) follow the same pattern: `NotificationAdapter` interface, pluggable transport, `LoggingTransport` fallback, error handling returning `NotificationResult`
- Preferences support all 3 configurable channels (email, SMS, webhook) with per-channel enabled/disabled and per-category subscriptions. `in_app` is always allowed.
- History service captures all notification delivery attempts via EventBus event subscriptions (`notification:sent`, `notification:failed`)
- Event-driven architecture is consistent throughout: services emit domain events, NotificationService subscribes and dispatches

### System Architecture Summary

**Notification System** — Event-driven, multi-channel notification delivery with user preferences and history tracking.

**Flow**: Domain Event → EventBus → NotificationService → PreferencesService (check) → Adapter (deliver) → EventBus (result) → NotificationHistoryService (log)

**Components**:
- **Models**: `Notification`, `NotificationPayload`, `NotificationAdapter` interface, `UserPreferences` (multi-channel), `NotificationLog`
- **Adapters**: `EmailAdapter` (HTML templates, XSS-safe), `SmsAdapter` (160-char truncation), `WebhookAdapter` (JSON POST with Bearer auth) — all with pluggable transports
- **Services**: `NotificationService` (dispatch orchestrator), `PreferencesService` (per-channel opt-in/out), `NotificationHistoryService` (audit log)
- **Validators**: `order.validator.ts`, `user.validator.ts` (pure functions extracted from services)
- **Infrastructure**: `EventBus`, `BaseRepository`, `Database`, `Logger`, `Pagination`

**Known limitations**:
- `EmailPreferences.frequency` field (immediate/daily/weekly) is stored but not enforced — digest batching is not implemented
- No retry logic in adapters — retries can be added at the transport level
- `CallbackRegistry` (`src/utils/callback-registry.ts`) is unused but still present — safe to delete
- No `in_app` adapter implementation exists — `in_app` notifications are stored as `pending`
- Notification storage is in-memory (via `BaseRepository`) — not persisted across restarts

### Files changed
- `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts` (removed stale comment)
- `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` (deleted)

### Build status
- TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` passes)
- All 225 tests pass across 15 test files
